topic: Six months ago I was a photographer. Today I run an AI agency.

slides.title: Photographer to AI agency founder in six months.
That sounds like a pivot. It was not.
slides.body: For 10 years I ran a photography business in London. Every part of it: the shoots, the editing, the bookings, the follow-ups, the social media, the emails, the invoices. All of it, by myself.

slides.title: I built systems to survive.
slides.body: Automations that sent reminders, followed up with leads, kept my calendar full without me sitting at a screen all day.

slides.title: Then I realised something.
slides.body: Every small business owner I knew had the same problem. Restaurants missing calls during service. Clinics losing patients to forgotten appointments. Salons with incredible work and empty Instagram feeds.

slides.title: They did not need more marketing.
slides.body: They needed the invisible work handled.

slides.title: So I started building it for them.
slides.body: Voice agents that answer every call. Follow-up systems that bring customers back. Content engines that keep the feed alive.

slides.title: The photography taught me what small businesses actually need.
slides.body: Not another tool. Someone who has lived it and built the fix themselves.

slides.title: If the admin is eating your time, send me a message.
slides.body: I will show you what we build.
